parsed_output.justification: The transcript shows that Mitchell confidently claims to be the Trouble Maker, confirming that his role does not belong to the Werewolf team. Mike clearly asserts he was the Robber and then performs a card‐swap, which keeps him aligned with the Villager side. Both of these roles cannot be Werewolves. As a result, the only plausible candidate for a Werewolf among the remaining players—Justin and Julie—must be one of them. Given Julie’s vague comments and lack of any direct claim to a neutral role, while Justin consistently suspects others and implicates himself repeatedly, he is the most suspicious. Additionally, Justin’s particular statements (“Don’t trust him. He’s a werewolf.” and recurring doubt around his own alignment) suggest he may have lied, increasing the likelihood that Justin is in fact the Werewolf. Thus, Justin is the most probable werewolf in this game.

validation.justification: The transcript shows that Mitchell confidently claims to be the Trouble Maker, confirming that his role does not belong to the Werewolf team. Mike clearly asserts he was the Robber and then performs a card‐swap, which keeps him aligned with the Villager side. Both of these roles cannot be Werewolves. As a result, the only plausible candidate for a Werewolf among the remaining players—Justin and Julie—must be one of them. Given Julie’s vague comments and lack of any direct claim to a neutral role, while Justin consistently suspects others and implicates himself repeatedly, he is the most suspicious. Additionally, Justin’s particular statements (“Don’t trust him. He’s a werewolf.” and recurring doubt around his own alignment) suggest he may have lied, increasing the likelihood that Justin is in fact the Werewolf. Thus, Justin is the most probable werewolf in this game.
